stories.description: - Firebase Emulator Setup + Firestore Cost-Saving Guards

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: `firebase.json` contains an `emulators` block with `auth.port` = 9099 and `firestore.port` = 8080
`server/auth.ts` logs `"Firebase Auth Emulator active"` when `FIREBASE_AUTH_EMULATOR_HOST` env var is set
`server/firestore.ts` logs `"Firestore Emulator active"` when `FIRESTORE_EMULATOR_HOST` env var is set
`services/firebase.ts` calls `connectAuthEmulator` when `VITE_USE_FIREBASE_EMULATOR === "true"`
`.env.emulator` file exists with `FIREBASE_AUTH_EMULATOR_HOST`, `FIRESTORE_EMULATOR_HOST`, and `VITE_USE_FIREBASE_EMULATOR` variables
Zero `onSnapshot` calls exist on collection-level queries in the codebase (grep verification — already passing, formalize as regression gate)
All server-side Firestore reads are single-doc `.doc(id).get()` or have `.limit()` applied (grep verification)

stories.description: - Remove Driver Pay Nav + Role-Based Nav Visibility

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: The string `"Driver Pay"` does not appear as a nav label in the `categories` array in `App.tsx`
`LEGACY_TAB_ALIASES` in `App.tsx` does not contain entries mapping to `"driver-pay"` (0 occurrences)
The `activeTab === "driver-pay"` rendering block is removed from `App.tsx` (0 occurrences in render)
`NavItem` interface in `App.tsx` includes `roles?: UserRole[]` field (Grep for `roles\?:` in `NavItem` returns 1 match)
`operations-hub` nav item includes a `roles` array that does NOT contain `"driver"` or `"DRIVER_PORTAL"`
`loads` (Load Board) nav item has no `roles` restriction OR its `roles` array includes both `"dispatcher"` and `"driver"`
`filteredCategories` filter function checks `item.roles` and returns `false` when `user.role` is not in the array (admin bypasses)
`npx vitest run src/__tests__/components/App.navigation.test.tsx` exits with code 0 and all assertions pass
`filteredCategories` fails when `user.role === "driver"` attempts to access `operations-hub` -- returns 0 items matching that `id` (role-gated item filtered out)
A test renders `SafetyView` with `getCompanyUsers` returning `[]` and asserts the `"Non-Compliant"` KPI card displays `"N/A"` (not a hardcoded number like `"13"`)
A test renders `SafetyView` with `getEquipment` returning `[]` and asserts the `"Out of Service"` KPI card displays `"N/A"` (not a hardcoded number)
With 1 operator having `totalScore < 70`, the Non-Compliant KPI displays `"1"` not `"N/A"` (positive case, covered by existing R-P6-01 test)
Grep for hardcoded `"13"` in `SafetyView.tsx` KPI rendering returns 0 matches -- rejects hardcoded compliance counts

stories.description: - Team Tab Slide-Out Profile Editor with Inline Quick Edit

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: `EditUserModal.tsx` outer wrapper uses `right-0` and `inset-y-0` CSS classes (not `inset-0` with centered flex)
Panel outer div contains CSS classes `w-full` and `md:max-w-2xl` -- below 768px viewport the panel renders at 100% width
`screen.getByText("Identity")`, `screen.getByText("Pay Profile")`, and `screen.getByText("Access")` all return truthy in a render of `UserProfilePanel`
`CompanyProfile` registry tab renders 1 `<select>` element with `aria-label` containing `"role"` per user row
Changing the inline role `<select>` to `"dispatcher"` calls `updateUser` with `expect.objectContaining({ role: "dispatcher" })`
`CompanyProfile` registry tab renders 1 `<input type="number">` with `aria-label` containing `"pay rate"` per user row
Blurring the `payRate` input with value `50` calls `updateUser` with `expect.objectContaining({ payRate: 50 })`
Clicking the `ChevronRight` details button renders `UserProfilePanel` with `screen.getByText("Identity")` returning truthy
`import { EditUserModal } from "./EditUserModal"` resolves to `UserProfilePanel` (backward-compatible re-export)
`npx vitest run src/__tests__/components/EditUserModal.test.tsx` exits with code 0 after conversion
Entering `payRate` value `-1` in the inline input does NOT trigger `updateUser` (invalid input rejected)
